question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 76.0°C and 7.7 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 488.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1220.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using a steady-flow boiler model with negligible KE/PE changes and no shaft work:

- Energy balance:  
  \( q_{in} = h_{out} - h_{in} \)

- Entropy balance:  
  \( s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \dfrac{q_{in}}{T_H} \)

- Exergy destruction:  
  \( x_{dest} = T_0\, s_{gen} \)

- Second-law efficiency:  
  \[
  \eta_{II}
  =
  \frac{(h_{out}-h_{in})-T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})}
       {\left(1-\dfrac{T_0}{T_H}\right) q_{in}}
  \]

Using steam-table properties:

- Inlet, compressed liquid at \(76.0^\circ\mathrm{C}, 7.7\ \mathrm{MPa}\):  
  \(h_{in} \approx h_f(76^\circ\mathrm{C}) + v_f(76^\circ\mathrm{C})(P-P_{sat}) \)  
  \(s_{in} \approx s_f(76^\circ\mathrm{C})\)

- Outlet, superheated steam at \(7.7\ \mathrm{MPa}, 488.0^\circ\mathrm{C}\): from superheated steam tables.

Numerically:

- \( h_{in} \approx 325.8\ \mathrm{kJ/kg} \)
- \( s_{in} \approx 1.026\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)} \)
- \( h_{out} \approx 3370.0\ \mathrm{kJ/kg} \)
- \( s_{out} \approx 6.703\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)} \)

Then:

\[
q_{in}=3370.0-325.8=3044.2\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
s_{gen}=(6.703-1.026)-\frac{3044.2}{1220.0}=3.182\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

\[
x_{dest}=298.15(3.182)=948.8\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\eta_{II}
=
\frac{3044.2-298.15(6.703-1.026)}
{\left(1-\frac{298.15}{1220.0}\right)3044.2}
=0.588
\]

Final answers:

h_in = 325.8 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3370.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3044.2 kJ/kg  
s_in = 1.026 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 6.703 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 3.182 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 948.8 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.588
response_text:
Using a steady-flow boiler model with negligible KE/PE changes and no shaft work:

- Energy balance:  
  \( q_{in} = h_{out} - h_{in} \)

- Entropy balance:  
  \( s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \dfrac{q_{in}}{T_H} \)

- Exergy destruction:  
  \( x_{dest} = T_0\, s_{gen} \)

- Second-law efficiency:  
  \[
  \eta_{II}
  =
  \frac{(h_{out}-h_{in})-T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})}
       {\left(1-\dfrac{T_0}{T_H}\right) q_{in}}
  \]

Using steam-table properties:

- Inlet, compressed liquid at \(76.0^\circ\mathrm{C}, 7.7\ \mathrm{MPa}\):  
  \(h_{in} \approx h_f(76^\circ\mathrm{C}) + v_f(76^\circ\mathrm{C})(P-P_{sat}) \)  
  \(s_{in} \approx s_f(76^\circ\mathrm{C})\)

- Outlet, superheated steam at \(7.7\ \mathrm{MPa}, 488.0^\circ\mathrm{C}\): from superheated steam tables.

Numerically:

- \( h_{in} \approx 325.8\ \mathrm{kJ/kg} \)
- \( s_{in} \approx 1.026\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)} \)
- \( h_{out} \approx 3370.0\ \mathrm{kJ/kg} \)
- \( s_{out} \approx 6.703\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)} \)

Then:

\[
q_{in}=3370.0-325.8=3044.2\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
s_{gen}=(6.703-1.026)-\frac{3044.2}{1220.0}=3.182\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

\[
x_{dest}=298.15(3.182)=948.8\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\eta_{II}
=
\frac{3044.2-298.15(6.703-1.026)}
{\left(1-\frac{298.15}{1220.0}\right)3044.2}
=0.588
\]

Final answers:

h_in = 325.8 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3370.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3044.2 kJ/kg  
s_in = 1.026 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 6.703 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 3.182 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 948.8 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.588

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 51.0°C and 14.4 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 549.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 944.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using a steady-flow boiler model with negligible kinetic/potential energy changes and no shaft work:

- **Energy balance**:  
  \( q_{in} = h_{out} - h_{in} \)

- **Entropy balance** with heat transfer from a source at \(T_H=944.0\ \text{K}\):  
  \( s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \dfrac{q_{in}}{T_H} \)

- **Exergy destruction**:  
  \( x_{dest} = T_0\, s_{gen} \)

- **Second-law efficiency** for the boiler:  
  \[
  \eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta \psi}{\text{exergy of heat input}}
  =\frac{(h_{out}-h_{in})-T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})}{q_{in}\left(1-\dfrac{T_0}{T_H}\right)}
  \]

## 1) Inlet state: compressed liquid, 51.0°C, 14.4 MPa
For compressed liquid water, use the saturated-liquid approximation at the same temperature with pressure correction:
\[
h_{in}\approx h_f(51^\circ\text{C})+v_f(51^\circ\text{C})\,[P-P_{sat}(51^\circ\text{C})]
\]
and
\[
s_{in}\approx s_f(51^\circ\text{C})
\]

This gives approximately:
- \(h_{in}\approx 228.2\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(s_{in}\approx 0.715\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

## 2) Exit state: superheated steam, 14.4 MPa, 549.0°C
From superheated steam tables at \(P=14.4\ \text{MPa}\), \(T=549.0^\circ\text{C}\):
- \(h_{out}\approx 3448.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(s_{out}\approx 6.530\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

## 3) Heat input
\[
q_{in}=3448.0-228.2=3219.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

## 4) Entropy generation
\[
s_{gen}=(6.530-0.715)-\frac{3219.8}{944.0}
\]
\[
s_{gen}=5.815-3.411=2.404\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

## 5) Exergy destruction
\[
x_{dest}=298.15\times 2.404=716.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

## 6) Second-law efficiency
First, the useful exergy increase of the water:
\[
\Delta\psi=(3448.0-228.2)-298.15(6.530-0.715)
\]
\[
\Delta\psi=3219.8-1733.7=1486.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Exergy transferred with heat:
\[
q_{in}\left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right)=3219.8\left(1-\frac{298.15}{944.0}\right)=2202.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Thus,
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{1486.1}{2202.3}=0.675
\]

h_in = 228.2 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3448.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3219.8 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.715 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 6.530 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 2.404 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 716.6 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.675

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 1.2 MPa. Stream 1: 179.0°C, 1.1 kg/s. Stream 2: 48.0°C, 7.9 kg/s. Find the exit temperature.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
raw_response:
For a steady, adiabatic mixing chamber with negligible kinetic and potential energy changes,

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

and

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2
\]

At \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\), the saturation temperature is about \(188^\circ\text{C}\), so both streams are compressed liquids. For compressed liquid water, use the standard approximation:

\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)
\]

From saturated-liquid water properties:
- \(h_1 \approx h_f(179.0^\circ\text{C}) = 758.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(h_2 \approx h_f(48.0^\circ\text{C}) = 201.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3=1.1+7.9=9.0\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:
\[
h_3=\frac{\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2}{\dot m_3}
=\frac{(1.1)(758.8)+(7.9)(201.0)}{9.0}
=269.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Then from compressed-liquid/saturated-liquid properties,
\[
h_3 \approx h_f(T_3)
\]
which gives
\[
T_3 \approx 64.3^\circ\text{C}
\]

h_1 = 758.8 kJ/kg
h_2 = 201.0 kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = 9.0 kg/s
h_3 = 269.2 kJ/kg
T_3 = 64.3 °C

\]

question:
In a mixing chamber, stream 1 (water at 181.0°C, 1.4 MPa, 8.5 kg/s) mixes with stream 2 (water at 50.0°C, 1.4 MPa, 8.9 kg/s). The mixing is adiabatic at constant pressure. Determine the exit temperature and mass flow rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
raw_response:
At 1.4 MPa, the saturation temperature of water is about 195°C, so both inlet streams are compressed liquids. For compressed liquid water, a standard approximation is

- \( h(T,p) \approx h_f(T) \)

Thus, from water tables:

- \( h_1 \approx h_f(181.0^\circ\text{C}) \approx 767.0 \,\text{kJ/kg} \)
- \( h_2 \approx h_f(50.0^\circ\text{C}) \approx 209.3 \,\text{kJ/kg} \)

For an adiabatic mixing chamber at steady state:

1. Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3 = \dot m_1 + \dot m_2 = 8.5 + 8.9 = 17.4 \ \text{kg/s}
\]

2. Energy balance:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1 + \dot m_2 h_2 = \dot m_3 h_3
\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(8.5)(767.0)+(8.9)(209.3)}{17.4}=481.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now find the exit temperature corresponding to compressed liquid water at \(h_3 \approx 481.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\). Using the saturated-liquid table as an approximation gives:

\[
T_3 \approx 114.8^\circ\text{C}
\]

\]

\]

From compressed-liquid water properties at \(2.0\ \text{MPa}\), \(h_3 \approx 372.3\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to:

\[
T_3 \approx 88.4^\circ\text{C}
\]

question:
Water at 179.0°C (1.9 kg/s) mixes adiabatically with water at 45.0°C (5.8 kg/s) at a constant pressure of 0.9 MPa. Find the exit temperature and entropy generation rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
At \(P=0.9\ \text{MPa}\), the saturation temperature is about \(175.4^\circ\text{C}\). So:

- State 1 (\(179^\circ\text{C}\)) is **slightly superheated vapor**
- State 2 (\(45^\circ\text{C}\)) is **compressed liquid**
- The mixer is adiabatic and operates at steady state

Using steam-table properties:

- For state 1 at \(0.9\ \text{MPa},\ 179^\circ\text{C}\):  
  \(h_1 \approx 2780.6\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1 \approx 6.645\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

- For state 2 at \(45^\circ\text{C}\) (compressed-liquid approximation):  
  \(h_2 \approx h_f(45^\circ\text{C}) = 188.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\)  
  \(s_2 \approx s_f(45^\circ\text{C}) = 0.638\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.9+5.8=7.7\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]
\[
h_3=\frac{(1.9)(2780.6)+(5.8)(188.4)}{7.7}=827.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(0.9\ \text{MPa}\), this enthalpy lies between \(h_f\) and \(h_g\), so the exit is a **saturated mixture**, and:
\[
T_3=T_{sat}(0.9\ \text{MPa})\approx 175.4^\circ\text{C}
\]

Using saturated-mixture relations at \(0.9\ \text{MPa}\), the exit entropy is:
\[
s_3 \approx 2.292\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy rate balance for an adiabatic mixer:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}=(7.7)(2.292)-(1.9)(6.645)-(5.8)(0.638)=1.32\ \text{kW/K}
\]

h_1 = 2780.6 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 188.4 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 7.7 kg/s  
h_3 = 827.4 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 175.4 °C  
s_1 = 6.645 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.638 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 2.292 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 1.32 kW/K

question:

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
At \(P=1.5\text{ MPa}\), the saturation temperature is about \(198.3^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams and the exit stream are **compressed liquids**.

Using the usual compressed-liquid approximation for water:

\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T), \qquad s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)
\]

From saturated-liquid water properties at the given temperatures:

- At \(146.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 614.6\text{ kJ/kg},\; s_1 \approx 1.810\text{ kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- At \(37.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 155.0\text{ kJ/kg},\; s_2 \approx 0.532\text{ kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Mass balance:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=3.0+3.0=6.0\text{ kg/s}
\]

Energy balance for an adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

\[
h_3=\frac{3h_1+3h_2}{6}=\frac{h_1+h_2}{2}\approx 384.8\text{ kJ/kg}
\]

From compressed-liquid/saturated-liquid properties, this corresponds to:

\[
T_3 \approx 91.9^\circ\text{C}, \qquad s_3 \approx 1.213\text{ kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy rate balance for the adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=6(1.213)-3(1.810)-3(0.532)\approx 0.255\text{ kW/K}
\]

From saturated-liquid water tables (interpolated):

- At \(36.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 150.8\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2 \approx 0.519\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

\[

\]

\[

\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(6.3)(653.6)+(5.0)(150.8)}{11.3}\approx 431.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

From compressed-liquid/saturated-liquid tables, \(h_3 \approx 431.1\ \text{kJ/kg}\) gives:

\[
T_3 \approx 102.9^\circ\text{C}, \qquad s_3 \approx 1.339\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy rate balance for an adiabatic steady mixer:

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=(11.3)(1.339)-(6.3)(1.892)-(5.0)(0.519)\approx 0.616\ \text{kW/K}
\]

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.2 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 160.0°C, 1.8 kg/s. Stream 2: 76.0°C, 4.9 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady, adiabatic mixing at \(P=0.2\ \text{MPa}\), with negligible KE/PE changes:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2
\]

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

For entropy:

\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

For exergy destruction:

\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0 \dot S_{gen}
\]

And the mixing-chamber second-law efficiency is taken as

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot m_3 \psi_3}{\dot m_1 \psi_1+\dot m_2 \psi_2},
\qquad
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]

Using steam-table properties:

- Stream 1 at \(0.2\ \text{MPa},\ 160^\circ\text{C}\): superheated vapor  
- Stream 2 at \(0.2\ \text{MPa},\ 76^\circ\text{C}\): compressed liquid, approximated by saturated liquid at \(76^\circ\text{C}\)
- Dead state \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K},\ P_0=0.1\ \text{MPa}\), with \(h_0 \approx 104.86\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_0 \approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

At \(0.2\ \text{MPa}\), the mixed-state enthalpy falls between \(h_f\) and \(h_g\), so the exit is a saturated mixture and therefore:

\[
T_3=T_{sat}(0.2\ \text{MPa})\approx 120.2^\circ\text{C}
\]

Final results:

h_1 = 2787.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 318.2 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.7 kg/s  
h_3 = 981.5 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 120.2 °C  
s_1 = 7.320 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 1.027 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 2.742 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.163 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 48.5 kW  
η_II = 0.959

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.5 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 102.0°C, 2.4 kg/s. Stream 2: 58.0°C, 4.0 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Treat the water as a compressed liquid at 0.5 MPa, so use the standard approximation

- \( h \approx c_p T_{(^\circ C)} \)
- \( s \approx c_p \ln\!\left(\dfrac{T}{273.15}\right) \)

with \( c_p \approx 4.18 \,\text{kJ/(kg·K)} \).

Steady, adiabatic mixing chamber:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=2.4+4.0=6.4\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

So \(T_3\) is the mass-weighted average temperature for liquid water:

\[
T_3=\frac{\dot m_1 T_1+\dot m_2 T_2}{\dot m_3}
=\frac{2.4(102)+4.0(58)}{6.4}=74.5^\circ\text{C}
\]

Entropy balance for an adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

Exergy destruction:

\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0 \dot S_{gen}
\qquad\text{with}\qquad T_0=298.15\text{ K}
\]

Second-law efficiency for the mixer:

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot E_{x,out}}{\dot E_{x,in}}
\]

with flow exergy (neglecting KE/PE, and the small liquid pressure term which nearly cancels since all streams are at the same pressure):

\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]

using \(h_0 \approx 4.18(25)=104.5\ \text{kJ/kg}\) and
\(s_0 \approx 4.18\ln(298.15/273.15)=0.3661\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\).

Final answers:

h_1 = 426.36 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 242.44 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.40 kg/s  
h_3 = 311.41 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 74.50 °C  
s_1 = 1.3268 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.8049 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.0081 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.0480 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 14.31 kW  
η_II = 0.874

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 1.2 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 237.0°C, 4.9 kg/s. Stream 2: 33.0°C, 4.2 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady, adiabatic mixing at the same pressure:

- Mass balance:  
  \[
  \dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=4.9+4.2=9.1\ \text{kg/s}
  \]

- Energy balance:  
  \[
  \dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
  \]

- Steam-table properties used:
  - At \(P=1.2\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=237^\circ\text{C}\) (superheated):  
    \(h_1 \approx 2908.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1 \approx 6.781\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
  - For stream 2 (\(33^\circ\text{C}\), compressed liquid), using the compressed-liquid approximation:  
    \(h_2 \approx h_f(33^\circ\text{C})+v_f(P-P_{sat}) \approx 139.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\)  
    \(s_2 \approx s_f(33^\circ\text{C}) \approx 0.4776\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then
\[
h_3=\frac{\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2}{\dot m_3}
=\frac{(4.9)(2908.0)+(4.2)(139.4)}{9.1}
\approx 1630.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\), this \(h_3\) lies in the saturated-mixture region, so:
\[
T_3=T_{sat}(1.2\ \text{MPa})\approx 188.2^\circ\text{C}
\]

Using saturated properties at \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\):
\[
h_f \approx 798.3,\quad h_{fg}\approx 1985.0,\quad
s_f\approx 2.217,\quad s_{fg}\approx 4.303
\]
\[
x_3=\frac{h_3-h_f}{h_{fg}}\approx \frac{1630.2-798.3}{1985.0}\approx 0.419
\]
\[
s_3=s_f+x_3 s_{fg}\approx 4.020\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy generation rate:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}\approx (9.1)(4.020)-(4.9)(6.781)-(4.2)(0.4776)
\approx 1.35\ \text{kW/K}
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}=(298.15)(1.35)\approx 404\ \text{kW}
\]

Second-law efficiency, using
\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]
with dead-state water at \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C},\ P_0=0.1\ \text{MPa}\):  
\(h_0\approx 104.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_0\approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

This gives approximately:
\[
\psi_1\approx 890.9,\quad \psi_2\approx 1.62,\quad \psi_3\approx 436.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot m_3\psi_3}{\dot m_1\psi_1+\dot m_2\psi_2}\approx 0.908
\]

h_1 = 2908.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 139.4 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 9.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 1630.2 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 188.2 °C  
s_1 = 6.781 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.4776 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 4.020 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 1.35 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 404 kW  
η_II = 0.908

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.7 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 130.0°C, 7.8 kg/s. Stream 2: 50.0°C, 9.3 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Since \(T_1=130^\circ\text{C}\) and \(T_2=50^\circ\text{C}\) are both below \(T_{\text{sat}}(0.7\ \text{MPa})\approx 165^\circ\text{C}\), all streams are compressed liquid water. Using the standard compressed-liquid approximation,
\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T),\qquad s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)
\]
(with a very small pressure correction to \(h\) at 0.7 MPa).

Steady adiabatic mixing:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=7.8+9.3=17.1\ \text{kg/s}
\]
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]
Using liquid-water properties:
- at \(130^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1\approx 546.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1\approx 1.634\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- at \(50^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2\approx 210.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2\approx 0.7038\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then:
\[
h_3=\frac{\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2}{\dot m_3}\approx 363.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
which corresponds to
\[
T_3\approx 86.7^\circ\text{C},\qquad s_3\approx 1.154\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy balance for adiabatic mixing:
\[
\dot S_{\rm gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\approx 0.443\ \text{kW/K}
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{\rm dest}=T_0\dot S_{\rm gen}
=(298.15\ \text{K})(0.443)\approx 132\ \text{kW}
\]

For the second-law efficiency, use
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot m_3 \psi_3}{\dot m_1 \psi_1+\dot m_2 \psi_2}
=1-\frac{\dot X_{\rm dest}}{\dot X_{\rm in}}
\]
with
\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0),
\]
and dead-state liquid-water properties at \(25^\circ\text{C}, 0.1\ \text{MPa}\):
\[
h_0\approx 104.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_0\approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}.
\]
This gives
\[
\eta_{II}\approx 0.758.
\]

h_2 = 210.0 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 17.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 363.7 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 86.7 °C

Since \(T_1=130^\circ\text{C}\) and \(T_2=50^\circ\text{C}\) are both below \(T_{\text{sat}}(0.7\ \text{MPa})\approx 165^\circ\text{C}\), all streams are compressed liquid water. Using the standard compressed-liquid approximation,
\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T),\qquad s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)
\]
(with a very small pressure correction to \(h\) at 0.7 MPa).

Steady adiabatic mixing:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=7.8+9.3=17.1\ \text{kg/s}
\]
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]
Using liquid-water properties:
- at \(130^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1\approx 546.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1\approx 1.634\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- at \(50^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2\approx 210.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2\approx 0.7038\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then:
\[
h_3=\frac{\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2}{\dot m_3}\approx 363.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
which corresponds to
\[
T_3\approx 86.7^\circ\text{C},\qquad s_3\approx 1.154\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy balance for adiabatic mixing:
\[
\dot S_{\rm gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\approx 0.443\ \text{kW/K}
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{\rm dest}=T_0\dot S_{\rm gen}
=(298.15\ \text{K})(0.443)\approx 132\ \text{kW}
\]

For the second-law efficiency, use
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot m_3 \psi_3}{\dot m_1 \psi_1+\dot m_2 \psi_2}
=1-\frac{\dot X_{\rm dest}}{\dot X_{\rm in}}
\]
with
\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0),
\]
and dead-state liquid-water properties at \(25^\circ\text{C}, 0.1\ \text{MPa}\):
\[
h_0\approx 104.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_0\approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}.
\]
This gives
\[
\eta_{II}\approx 0.758.
\]

h_1 = 546.9 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 210.0 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 17.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 363.7 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 86.7 °C  
s_1 = 1.634 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.7038 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.154 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.443 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 132 kW  
η_II = 0.758

h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using the adiabatic nozzle energy balance and nozzle isentropic efficiency:

1. From the superheated-steam state at \(P_1 = 1.4\ \text{MPa},\ T_1 = 389.0^\circ\text{C}\), obtain \(h_1\) and \(s_1\).
2. For the isentropic exit state at \(P_2 = 0.7\ \text{MPa}\), set \(s_{2s}=s_1\) and read \(h_{2s}\).
3. Apply nozzle efficiency:
\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.91
\]
4. Then use the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]
with \(V_1=26.0\ \text{m/s}\).

This gives:

s_1 = 7.2690 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 3047.9 kJ/kg

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 278.0°C, 4.1 MPa with a velocity of 40.0 m/s and exits at 0.56 MPa. The nozzle isentropic efficiency is 93.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
For an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1 + \frac{V_1^2}{2} = h_2 + \frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

and the nozzle isentropic efficiency is

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

### 1) Inlet state
At \(P_1=4.1\text{ MPa},\; T_1=278.0^\circ\text{C}\), steam is **superheated**.  
From superheated steam tables (interpolated):

\[
h_1 \approx 2888 \text{ kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 6.22 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

### 2) Isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.56\text{ MPa}\)
For the isentropic case,

\[
s_{2s}=s_1
\]

At \(0.56\text{ MPa}\), using saturated properties (interpolated), the state falls in the wet region, so

\[
x_{2s}=\frac{s_1-s_f}{s_{fg}}
\]

and then

\[
h_{2s}=h_f+x_{2s}h_{fg}
\]

This gives

\[
h_{2s}\approx 2512\text{ kJ/kg}
\]

### 3) Actual exit enthalpy using nozzle efficiency
\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2=2888-0.93(2888-2512)\approx 2538\text{ kJ/kg}
\]

### 4) Exit velocity
Using the steady-flow energy equation:

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)(1000)}
\]

with \(V_1=40.0\text{ m/s}\):

\[
V_2=\sqrt{40.0^2+2(2888-2538)(1000)}
\approx 8.36\times 10^2 \text{ m/s}
\]

So the exit velocity is about **836 m/s**.

h_1 = 2888 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.22 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2512 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 836 m/s  
h_2 = 2538 kJ/kg

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 276.0°C, 0.5 MPa with V₁ = 18.0 m/s and exits at 0.15 MPa. The nozzle efficiency is 98.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Use the steady, adiabatic nozzle relation and the nozzle isentropic efficiency:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.98
\]

For state 1, from superheated steam properties at \(P_1=0.5\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=276^\circ\text{C}\):

- \(h_1 \approx 3016.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(s_1 \approx 7.360\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

For the isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.15\ \text{MPa}\), set \(s_{2s}=s_1\). Interpolating in the superheated region gives:

- \(h_{2s} \approx 2750.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Then the actual exit enthalpy is:

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2=3016.0-0.98(3016.0-2750.0)=2755.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now apply the energy equation for velocity:

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(1000)(h_1-h_2)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{18.0^2+2(1000)(3016.0-2755.3)}\approx 722.0\ \text{m/s}
\]

At \(P_2=0.15\ \text{MPa}\) and \(h_2=2755.3\ \text{kJ/kg}\), the exit entropy is approximately:

- \(s_2 \approx 7.372\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Since the nozzle is adiabatic, entropy generation per unit mass is:

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]

\[
s_{gen}=7.372-7.360=0.012\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_1 = 3016.0 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 7.360 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2750.0 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 722.0 m/s  
h_2 = 2755.3 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 7.372 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.012 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 293.0°C, 3.7 MPa with V₁ = 62.0 m/s and exits at 0.42 MPa. The nozzle efficiency is 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using steam tables:

1. **Inlet state** at \(P_1=3.7\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=293.0^\circ\text{C}\) is **superheated steam**.  
   From superheated steam tables (interpolated):
   \[
   h_1 \approx 2947.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_1 \approx 6.3760\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

2. **Isentropic exit state** at \(P_2=0.42\ \text{MPa}\):  
   \[
   s_{2s}=s_1
   \]
   At \(0.42\ \text{MPa}\) (saturated table, interpolated):
   \[
   h_f \approx 611.8,\quad h_{fg}\approx 2128.7,\quad s_f\approx 1.7934,\quad s_{fg}\approx 5.0873
   \]
   Since \(s_1 < s_g\), the isentropic exit is a wet mixture:
   \[
   x_s=\frac{s_1-s_f}{s_{fg}}=\frac{6.3760-1.7934}{5.0873}\approx 0.9008
   \]
   \[
   h_{2s}=h_f+x_s h_{fg}\approx 611.8+(0.9008)(2128.7)\approx 2529.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

3. **Nozzle efficiency**:
   \[
   \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.93
   \]
   \[
   h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
   \]
   \[
   h_2=2947.0-0.93(2947.0-2529.3)\approx 2558.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Exit velocity** from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
   \[
   h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
   \]
   \[
   V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)(1000)}
   \]
   \[
   V_2=\sqrt{62.0^2+2(2947.0-2558.5)(1000)}\approx 883.8\ \text{m/s}
   \]

5. **Actual exit entropy** at \(P_2=0.42\ \text{MPa}\):  
   Since \(h_2<h_g\), the actual exit is also a wet mixture.
   \[
   x=\frac{h_2-h_f}{h_{fg}}=\frac{2558.5-611.8}{2128.7}\approx 0.9145
   \]
   \[
   s_2=s_f+x s_{fg}\approx 1.7934+(0.9145)(5.0873)\approx 6.4457\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

6. **Entropy generation per unit mass** for an adiabatic nozzle:
   \[
   s_{gen}=s_2-s_1=6.4457-6.3760\approx 0.0697\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

question:
Steam at 451.0°C / 4.1 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 70.0 m/s and exits at 0.62 MPa (η_nozzle = 92.0%). The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using the steady, adiabatic nozzle model:

- Energy:
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

- Nozzle isentropic efficiency:
\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.92
\]

- Adiabatic entropy generation per unit mass:
\[
s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1
\]

- Exergy destruction per unit mass:
\[
x_{\text{dest}}=T_0\,s_{\text{gen}}
\]

- Second-law efficiency for a nozzle:
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke + x_{\text{dest}}}
=\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{\text{dest}}}
\]

Using superheated-steam property interpolation:

- At \(P_1=4.1\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=451.0^\circ\text{C}\):
  \[
  h_1 \approx 3315.1\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad s_1 \approx 6.8996\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

- For the isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.62\ \text{MPa}\) with \(s_{2s}=s_1\):
  \[
  h_{2s} \approx 2823.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]

Then:

\[
h_2 = h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
=3315.1-0.92(3315.1-2823.1)
\approx 2862.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Exit velocity:

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)\times 1000}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{70.0^2+2(3315.1-2862.5)\times 1000}
\approx 953.6\ \text{m/s}
\]

At \(P_2=0.62\ \text{MPa},\ h_2=2862.5\ \text{kJ/kg}\), the exit entropy is:

\[
s_2 \approx 6.9715\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

So:

\[
s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1=6.9715-6.8996=0.0719\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

\[
x_{\text{dest}}=T_0 s_{\text{gen}}=(298.15)(0.0719)\approx 21.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{\text{dest}}}
=\frac{452.6}{452.6+21.4}\approx 0.955
\]

h_1 = 3315.1 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.8996 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2823.1 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 953.6 m/s  
h_2 = 2862.5 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 6.9715 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Steam at 315.0°C / 1.6 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 12.0 m/s and exits at 0.38 MPa (η_nozzle = 97.0%). The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steam tables and the nozzle/exergy balances:

1. **Inlet state** at \(P_1=1.6\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=315^\circ\text{C}\)  
   \(\rightarrow\) superheated steam:
   \[
   h_1 \approx 3077.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_1 \approx 6.955\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

2. **Isentropic exit state** at \(P_2=0.38\ \text{MPa}\) with \(s_{2s}=s_1\):  
   from the superheated/saturation tables at \(0.38\ \text{MPa}\),
   \[
   h_{2s} \approx 2748.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

3. **Nozzle efficiency**
   \[
   \eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
   \]
   so
   \[
   h_2=h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
   \]
   \[
   h_2=3077.0-0.97(3077.0-2748.0)=2757.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Exit velocity** from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
   \[
   h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
   \]
   \[
   V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)\times 1000}
   \]
   with \(V_1=12.0\ \text{m/s}\):
   \[
   V_2\approx 799\ \text{m/s}
   \]

5. **Actual exit entropy** at \(P_2=0.38\ \text{MPa},\ h_2=2757.9\ \text{kJ/kg}\)  
   \(\rightarrow\) slightly superheated:
   \[
   s_2 \approx 6.978\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

6. **Entropy generation** for an adiabatic one-inlet/one-outlet nozzle:
   \[
   s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1=6.978-6.955=0.023\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

7. **Exergy destruction**
   \[
   x_{\text{dest}}=T_0\,s_{\text{gen}}
   \]
   with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\):
   \[
   x_{\text{dest}}=298.15(0.023)=6.86\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

8. **Second-law efficiency** for the nozzle, using the exergy-product/exergy-fuel definition:
   \[
   \eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{\text{dest}}}
   \]
   where
   \[
   \Delta ke=h_1-h_2=319.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]
   hence
   \[
   \eta_{II}=\frac{319.1}{319.1+6.86}=0.979
   \]

Final answers:

h_1 = 3077.0 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.955 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2748.0 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 799 m/s  
h_2 = 2757.9 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 6.978 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.023 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 6.86 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.979

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 622.0 K and 0.51 MPa with a velocity of 50.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.18 MPa and η_nozzle = 95.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using \(R = c_p\!\left(1-\frac{1}{k}\right)=1.005\left(1-\frac{1}{1.4}\right)=0.2871\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\),

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=622.0\left(\frac{0.18}{0.51}\right)^{0.285714}
=461.92\ \text{K}
\]

\[
h_1=c_pT_1=1.005(622.0)=625.11\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=1.005(461.92)=464.23\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2=625.11-0.95(625.11-464.23)=472.27\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2( h_1-h_2 )\times 1000}
=\sqrt{50.0^2+2(625.11-472.27)\times 1000}
=555.14\ \text{m/s}
\]

For entropy, a reference must be chosen for a constant-\(c_p\) ideal gas. Using \(s=0\) at \(T_{ref}=300\ \text{K},\ P_{ref}=100\ \text{kPa}\),

\[
s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_1}{T_{ref}}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_1}{P_{ref}}\right)
\]

\[
s_1=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{622.0}{300}\right)-0.2871\ln\!\left(\frac{510}{100}\right)
=0.2650\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_1 = 625.11 kJ/kg
s_1 = 0.2650 kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = 464.23 kJ/kg
V_2 = 555.14 m/s
h_2 = 472.27 kJ/kg

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 561.0 K and 0.49 MPa with a velocity of 98.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.15 MPa and η_nozzle = 94.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
For an adiabatic nozzle with ideal-gas air:

- \( h = c_p T \)
- Isentropic relation:  
  \[
  \frac{T_{2s}}{T_1}=\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Nozzle efficiency:  
  \[
  \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
  \]
- Energy equation:  
  \[
  h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
  \]
  with \(h\) in kJ/kg and \(V\) in m/s.

Given:
- \(T_1 = 561.0\ \text{K}\)
- \(P_1 = 0.49\ \text{MPa}\)
- \(P_2 = 0.15\ \text{MPa}\)
- \(V_1 = 98.0\ \text{m/s}\)
- \(c_p = 1.005\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- \(k = 1.4\)
- \(\eta_n = 0.94\)

1. Inlet enthalpy:
\[
h_1 = c_p T_1 = 1.005(561.0)=563.805\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

2. Gas constant:
\[
R = c_p\frac{k-1}{k}=1.005\frac{0.4}{1.4}=0.2871\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

3. Isentropic exit temperature:
\[
T_{2s}=561.0\left(\frac{0.15}{0.49}\right)^{0.4/1.4}\approx 400.01\ \text{K}
\]

4. Isentropic exit enthalpy:
\[
h_{2s}=c_p T_{2s}=1.005(400.01)\approx 402.01\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

5. Actual exit enthalpy from nozzle efficiency:
\[
h_2 = h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]
\[
h_2=563.805-0.94(563.805-402.01)\approx 411.72\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

6. Exit velocity:
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{98.0^2+2000(563.805-411.72)}\approx 560.16\ \text{m/s}
\]

For \(s_1\), an entropy reference must be chosen. Using the common reference \(T_{ref}=300\ \text{K}\), \(P_{ref}=100\ \text{kPa}\), \(s_{ref}=0\):
\[
s_1=c_p\ln\left(\frac{T_1}{T_{ref}}\right)-R\ln\left(\frac{P_1}{P_{ref}}\right)
\]
\[
s_1=1.005\ln\left(\frac{561.0}{300}\right)-0.2871\ln\left(\frac{490}{100}\right)\approx 0.173
\]

h_1 = 563.81 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.173 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 402.01 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 560.16 m/s  
h_2 = 411.72 kJ/kg

For an adiabatic nozzle with ideal-gas air:

- \( h = c_p T \)
- Isentropic relation:  
  \[
  \frac{T_{2s}}{T_1}=\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Nozzle efficiency:  
  \[
  \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
  \]
- Energy equation:  
  \[
  h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
  \]
  with \(h\) in kJ/kg and \(V\) in m/s.

Given:
- \(T_1 = 561.0\ \text{K}\)
- \(P_1 = 0.49\ \text{MPa}\)
- \(P_2 = 0.15\ \text{MPa}\)
- \(V_1 = 98.0\ \text{m/s}\)
- \(c_p = 1.005\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- \(k = 1.4\)
- \(\eta_n = 0.94\)

1. Inlet enthalpy:
\[
h_1 = c_p T_1 = 1.005(561.0)=563.805\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

2. Gas constant:
\[
R = c_p\frac{k-1}{k}=1.005\frac{0.4}{1.4}=0.2871\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

3. Isentropic exit temperature:
\[
T_{2s}=561.0\left(\frac{0.15}{0.49}\right)^{0.4/1.4}\approx 400.01\ \text{K}
\]

4. Isentropic exit enthalpy:
\[
h_{2s}=c_p T_{2s}=1.005(400.01)\approx 402.01\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

5. Actual exit enthalpy from nozzle efficiency:
\[
h_2 = h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]
\[
h_2=563.805-0.94(563.805-402.01)\approx 411.72\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

6. Exit velocity:
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{98.0^2+2000(563.805-411.72)}\approx 560.16\ \text{m/s}
\]

For \(s_1\), an entropy reference must be chosen. Using the common reference \(T_{ref}=300\ \text{K}\), \(P_{ref}=100\ \text{kPa}\), \(s_{ref}=0\):
\[
s_1=c_p\ln\left(\frac{T_1}{T_{ref}}\right)-R\ln\left(\frac{P_1}{P_{ref}}\right)
\]
\[
s_1=1.005\ln\left(\frac{561.0}{300}\right)-0.2871\ln\left(\frac{490}{100}\right)\approx 0.173
\]

h_1 = 563.81 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.173 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 402.01 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 560.16 m/s

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 846.0 K / 0.48 MPa with V₁ = 59.0 m/s. It exits at 0.2 MPa with η_nozzle = 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s

raw_response:
Using constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas relations:

- \(h_1 = c_p T_1\)
- Isentropic exit temperature:
  \[
  T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Nozzle efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
  \]
- Steady adiabatic nozzle energy equation:
  \[
  h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
  \]
- Entropy generation for an adiabatic nozzle:
  \[
  s_{gen}=s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
  \]

Numerically:

\[
h_1=1.005(846.0)=850.23\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
T_{2s}=846.0\left(\frac{0.2}{0.48}\right)^{0.4/1.4}=658.78\ \text{K}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=1.005(658.78)=662.07\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
=850.23-0.93(850.23-662.07)=675.24\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2( h_1-h_2 )\,1000}
=\sqrt{59.0^2+2(174.99)\,1000}
=594.52\ \text{m/s}
\]

\[
T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}=\frac{675.24}{1.005}=671.88\ \text{K}
\]

\[
s_{gen}=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{671.88}{846.0}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.2}{0.48}\right)
=0.0198\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Since no entropy reference state is specified, take the inlet as the entropy datum, so \(s_1=0\) and \(s_2=s_{gen}\).

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 622.0 K / 0.81 MPa with V₁ = 59.0 m/s. It exits at 0.12 MPa with η_nozzle = 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using ideal-gas, constant-\(c_p\) relations and taking the inlet state as the entropy datum for reporting (\(s_1=0\)):

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=622\left(\frac{0.12}{0.81}\right)^{0.4/1.4}
\approx 360.45\ \text{K}
\]

\[
h_1=c_p T_1=1.005(622)=625.11\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_p T_{2s}=1.005(360.45)\approx 362.25\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_{nozzle}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.93
\]

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_{nozzle}(h_1-h_{2s})
\approx 380.65\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Energy equation for the adiabatic nozzle:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2\,1000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2\,1000}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(1000)(h_1-h_2)}
\approx 701.7\ \text{m/s}
\]

Also,

\[
T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}\approx \frac{380.65}{1.005}=378.76\ \text{K}
\]

For an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]

\[
s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
\approx 0.0498\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

So the requested results are:

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) at 621.0 K / 0.83 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 93.0 m/s and exits at 0.28 MPa (η_nozzle = 94.0%). T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Calculate the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using ideal-gas air with constant specific heats and \(h = c_p T\):

1. Inlet enthalpy  
\[
h_1=c_pT_1=(1.005)(621.0)=624.105\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

2. Inlet entropy relative to \((T_0,P_0)\)  
\[
s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_1}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_1}{P_0}\right)
\]
\[
s_1=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{621.0}{298.15}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.83}{0.10}\right)=0.13004\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

3. Isentropic exit state  
\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=621.0\left(\frac{0.28}{0.83}\right)^{0.4/1.4}=455.26\ \text{K}
\]
\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=1.005(455.26)=457.53\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

4. Nozzle efficiency  
\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]
\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
=624.105-0.94(624.105-457.53)=467.53\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

5. Exit velocity from SFEE for adiabatic nozzle  
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2\,1000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2\,1000}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}
=\sqrt{93.0^2+2000(624.105-467.53)}
=567.28\ \text{m/s}
\]

6. Exit entropy  
First,
\[
T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}=\frac{467.53}{1.005}=465.20\ \text{K}
\]
Then,
\[
s_2=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_0}\right)
\]
\[
s_2=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{465.20}{298.15}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.28}{0.10}\right)
=0.15160\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

7. Entropy generation for adiabatic nozzle  
\[
s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1=0.15160-0.13004=0.02156\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

8. Exergy destruction  
\[
x_{\text{dest}}=T_0 s_{\text{gen}}=(298.15)(0.02156)=6.43\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

9. Second-law efficiency for a nozzle (exergetic efficiency)  
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{\text{dest}}}
\]
where
\[
\Delta ke=\frac{V_2^2-V_1^2}{2\,1000}=h_1-h_2=156.58\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
Thus,
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{156.58}{156.58+6.43}=0.9606
\]

h_1 = 624.11 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.13004 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 457.53 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 567.28 m/s  
h_2 = 467.53 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 0.15160 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.02156 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 6.43 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.9606

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) at 675.0 K / 0.52 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 53.0 m/s and exits at 0.15 MPa (η_nozzle = 97.0%). T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Calculate the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using \(h=c_pT\) and taking entropy relative to the dead state \((T_0,P_0)\):

\[
h=c_pT,\qquad s=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P}{P_0}\right)
\]

For the isentropic exit state:

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=675\left(\frac{0.15}{0.52}\right)^{0.4/1.4}
=473.20\ \text{K}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=1.005(473.20)=475.56\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\Rightarrow h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_1=1.005(675)=678.38\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_2=678.38-0.97(678.38-475.56)=481.65\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Energy balance for the adiabatic nozzle:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2\times 1000(h_1-h_2)}
=\sqrt{53^2+2(1000)(196.73)}
=629.49\ \text{m/s}
\]

Entropy at inlet and exit:

\[
s_1=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{675}{298.15}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.52}{0.1}\right)=0.3480
\]

\[
s_2=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{479.25}{298.15}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.15}{0.1}\right)=0.3608
\]

For an adiabatic one-in/one-out nozzle:

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1=0.0128\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

\[
x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}=298.15(0.0128)=3.81\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Exergy-based second-law efficiency of the nozzle:

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{dest}}
=\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{dest}}
=\frac{196.73}{196.73+3.81}=0.9810
\]

h_1 = 678.38 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.3480 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 475.56 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 629.49 m/s  
h_2 = 481.65 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 0.3608 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.0128 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 3.81 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.9810

s_{gen}=s_2-s_1=0.0128\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

\[
x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}=298.15(0.0128)=3.81\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Exergy-based second-law efficiency of the nozzle:

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{dest}}
=\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{dest}}
=\frac{196.73}{196.73+3.81}=0.9810
\]

h_1 = 678.38 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.3480 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 475.56 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 629.49 m/s  
h_2 = 481.65 kJ/kg
